values.content:
=Score each profile below for relevance to the goal: "{{ $json._goal }}"
Ideal lead: "{{ $json._ideal }}"

Return a JSON array. For each profile include:
- linkedIn: copy exactly from input
- firstName: copy from input (correct if obviously wrong)
- lastName: copy from input
- role: copy from input (correct if obviously wrong)
- company: copy from input (correct if obviously wrong)
- location: copy from input
- roleCategory: short label e.g. "VC Analyst", "VC Associate", "Founder", "Engineer", "Community Builder", "Other"
- relevanceScore: 0.0–1.0
    0.9–1.0 = Clearly matches the ideal lead
    0.7–0.8 = Strong signal, closely related
    0.5–0.6 = Possible fit, some signal
    0.3–0.4 = Weak or tangential
    0.0–0.2 = Not relevant
    Be strict. Default lower when uncertain.
- reachOutReason: 1 sentence using their actual name, role, company. E.g. "Alex is an analyst at Sequoia — perfectly aligned with your goal to connect with early-career VC professionals in SF."
- isJunk: true if no real person info, boilerplate profile, company page, or job listing

Profiles:
{{ JSON.stringify($json.batch) }}

parameters.content:
# 🎯 LinkedIn Lead Finder
**Pre-loaded for:** Young VC professionals in San Francisco

## Setup — 2 minutes
1. Open **⚙️ CONFIGURE HERE** (the yellow node)
2. Fill in the 5 fields
3. Click **Execute Workflow**
4. Results land in your Sheet + Email

---
**Volume:** ~100 profiles per run
10 queries × 3 pages × 10 results = 300 raw hits → ~100 real LinkedIn profiles after filtering

**Works for any search:**
Hacker houses · VC jobs · Hiring engineers · Career networking

## ⚙️ 5 Fields to Fill

**YOUR_EMAIL** — where results are sent

**SEARCH_GOAL** — one sentence describing who you want. The AI uses this to score every result.

**IDEAL_LEAD_DESCRIPTION** — your perfect lead in detail. More specific = better AI scoring.

**SEARCH_QUERIES** — one Google search per line.
Format: `site:linkedin.com/in "keyword" role OR city`
Add up to 20 lines for more volume.

**SHEET_URL** — your Google Sheet URL
(needs columns: First Name, Last Name, LinkedIn, Role, Company, Role Category, Location, Relevance Score, Reach Out Reason, Query Group, Is Duplicate, Date Added, Dedupe Key)

## 🔄 Loop Architecture

This node generates one item per (query × page) combination and feeds them into the loop one at a time.

**10 queries × 3 pages = 30 HTTP calls**

The loop ensures each request fires sequentially — more reliable than firing them all at once, and avoids rate-limit errors from the Google API.

## 🧹 Profile Extraction

Parses each Google result to pull:
- **Name** from LinkedIn title format
- **Role + Company** from title
- **Location** from snippet text
- **LinkedIn URL** (cleaned, /in/ only)

Company pages, job listings, and non-LinkedIn URLs are dropped here before hitting the AI — saves cost and improves quality.

parameters.name: User-Agent
parameters.value: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

parameters.content:
## 🤖 AI Scoring (GPT-4o-mini)

Profiles are batched in groups of 10 and sent to the AI, which:
- Validates and corrects name/role/company
- Scores 0.0–1.0 against YOUR goal
- Writes a personalised reach-out reason
- Flags junk results

**Only profiles scoring ≥ 0.5 are saved.**
Change this threshold in the Parse node if needed.

## 📊 Google Sheets

Uses **appendOrUpdate** matching on LinkedIn URL.

✅ New lead → added as new row
✅ Same lead seen again → row updated, not duplicated
✅ Safe to run daily with no cleanup needed

**Required sheet columns (exact names):**
First Name, Last Name, LinkedIn, Role, Company, Role Category, Location, Relevance Score, Reach Out Reason, Query Group, Is Duplicate, Date Added, Dedupe Key

## 💡 Query Examples

**Young VC in SF (default)**
`site:linkedin.com/in "analyst" "venture capital" "San Francisco"`

**Hacker house organizers**
`site:linkedin.com/in "hacker house" founder OR organizer`

**Hiring ML engineers**
`site:linkedin.com/in "ML engineer" "open to work" startup`

**Career networking**
`site:linkedin.com/in "product manager" Series A startup`

**Founder retreats**
`site:linkedin.com/in "founder retreat" host OR organizer`

Tip: Use `site:linkedin.com/in` (with /in/) to target personal profiles only and get cleaner results.

## 🔑 Google Custom Search Setup

You need two things: an **API Key** and a **Search Engine ID (cx)**.

---

**Step 1 — Get your API Key**
1. Go to → https://console.cloud.google.com
2. Create a new project (or use existing)
3. Search for **"Custom Search API"** and enable it
4. Go to **Credentials → Create Credentials → API Key**
5. Copy the key and paste it into the HTTP node URL replacing `key=...`

---

**Step 2 — Get your Search Engine ID (cx)**
1. Go to → https://programmablesearchengine.google.com
2. Click **"Add"** to create a new search engine
3. Under **"Sites to search"** → type `linkedin.com`
4. Give it any name, click Create
5. Click your engine → **"Customize"** → copy the **Search Engine ID**
6. Paste it into the HTTP node URL replacing `cx=...`

---

**Step 3 — Enable "Search the entire web"**
1. In your search engine settings → **"Basics"**
2. Toggle ON **"Search the entire web"**
3. This lets it search all of LinkedIn, not just one page

---

⚠️ **Free tier:** 100 queries/day free
This workflow makes 30 queries per run — so you get ~3 free runs/day.
Paid tier is $5 per 1,000 queries after that.
